Patch Set 3:

(75 comments)

Thanks for the patch.

A number of style issues to be fixed to match Lustre and upstream coding style.

There are also a number of places where checks are added for signed ints, but they should really be for unsigned values.  That is not clear from the code because many variables are declared as "int" type when they should never be negative.  Those should be fixed in this patch to return an error for (val < 0), instead of (val < INT_MIN) if it doesn't already, and also fixed a follow-on patch to declare the variable type as unsigned.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

I thought that we agreed to have a single conversion function that only took a char to specify default units.  Can you explain why you think you still need to provide a "multiplier" option?  From my reading of the code, it seemed pretty straight forward to handle the non-units multiplier in the caller instead of in the parsing function.

Patch Set 3:

> (1 comment)
 > 
 > I thought that we agreed to have a single conversion function that
 > only took a char to specify default units.  Can you explain why you
 > think you still need to provide a "multiplier" option?  From my
 > reading of the code, it seemed pretty straight forward to handle
 > the non-units multiplier in the caller instead of in the parsing
 > function.

I provided the multiplier option because within the helper, the multiplier is accounted for when determining wrapping. I think it is safer that way instead of relying on the caller to pay attention to it.

Because I decided it was safer, I couldn't provide a unit field that was simply a char (I'd have to make up a unit for pages for instance). What I can do, is provide a simple enumeration representing default units/conversions instead of the multiplier and internally convert that to the correct conversion factor.

parents.subject: LU-8048 mgc: use the same lu_env for mgc_fs_{set,clean}up

commit.subject: LU-7334 lprocfs: Refactored string to value helpers
commit.message:
LU-7334 lprocfs: Refactored string to value helpers

Refactored the string parsing helpers to correct
inconsistent behavior and added handling for s64
overflow/underflow. Removed the old helper
functions and refactored functions that called
them.

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Di Natale <dinatale2@llnl.gov>
Change-Id: I84f3b5d46d4e20bd09fe223589e1be96dc8f7468
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/16930
Reviewed-by: Christopher J. Morrone <morrone2@llnl.gov>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
